description: Dogs (bravo, delta, charlie) are spawned with --model haiku flag (hardcoded in resolveRoleAgentConfigCore). The haiku alias resolves to claude-haiku-4-5@20251001 which Claude Code cannot access on the Pulse/API-billing account. Dogs hang at 'There's an issue with the selected model' error with no tmux activity. The daemon has an inline reaper fallback, but orphan cleanup is blocked. Fix: either change the model ID format (@ vs -) or grant Pulse account access to haiku model.
title: Dog sessions fail: haiku model alias resolves to inaccessible claude-haiku-4-5@20251001 on Pulse account

Add --base-branch flag to gt convoy create so convoys can target a feature branch instead of main. Requires: 1) BaseBranch field in ConvoyFields (internal/beads/fields.go), 2) propagate through feedFirstReady in convoy_manager.go:431 so gt sling gets --base-branch=<convoy.BaseBranch> automatically. Without this, convoys targeting feat/extraction-review (or any feature branch) require manual --base-branch on every gt sling call. Context from rainier: 12 extraction review gap beads needed this.
title: convoy create should support --base-branch to target feature branches

description:
When CLAUDECODE=1 is set in the tmux global environment (via tmux set-environment -g), all new tmux sessions inherit it. Claude Code refuses to start in sessions where CLAUDECODE is set (nested session check). This causes witnesses to fail to start silently - gt witness start reports success but no tmux session is created.

Root cause: Something (possibly a previous Claude Code session or daemon initialization) set CLAUDECODE=1 in the tmux global environment.

Fix applied: tmux set-environment -g -u CLAUDECODE (removed from global env)

Prevention: The gt daemon or gt witness start should unset CLAUDECODE when spawning new tmux sessions, or the startup scripts should explicitly unset it from the tmux global env. Consider adding this check to gt boot triage.
title: CLAUDECODE=1 in tmux global env blocks witness/agent spawning

description:
gt doctor --fix deletes ~/.claude/settings.json when it thinks the file is 'stale', but this destroys user-configured hooks (DCG, rch) and mcpServers settings. 

The claude-settings check should either:
1. Merge its changes with existing user config instead of deleting/replacing
2. Only manage the keys it owns (e.g. gastown-specific settings) and leave user hooks untouched
3. At minimum, back up the file before deleting it

Currently every doctor --fix run requires manually restoring settings.json, which defeats the purpose of auto-fix.

Reproduction:
1. Add hooks to ~/.claude/settings.json (PreToolUse with dcg, rch)
2. Run gt doctor --fix
3. settings.json is deleted
4. DCG and rch hooks stop working for all new sessions
title: gt doctor --fix should not delete user hooks from ~/.claude/settings.json

description:
## Context
The mol-idea-to-plan formula produces PRDs and implementation beads, but has no validation step. We need two changes:

## Change 1: Add two sections to the PRD template

In the intake step's PRD template (currently lines 77-101 of mol-idea-to-plan.formula.toml), add two new sections after Goals and before Non-Goals:

```markdown
## Success Criteria
(Specific, testable criteria that prove the goals are met. Each criterion should be independently verifiable. Format as a numbered list.)

## How to Validate
(For each success criterion above, describe the concrete actions an agent or human would take to verify it. Include: where to navigate in the app, what to click or input, and what the expected result looks like. These steps will be used by the validation formula after all implementation is complete.)
```

## Change 2: Auto-create a validation bead in the create-beads step

In the create-beads step, after creating all implementation beads and wiring dependencies, add instructions to create a validation bead:

The validation bead should:
- Have title 'Validate: {{problem}}'
- Depend on ALL implementation beads (so the daemon won't dispatch it until everything is done)
- Have a description that instructs the executor to:
  1. Read the PRD and extract Success Criteria + How to Validate sections
  2. Start the local development stack
  3. For each criterion, execute the validation actions described in the PRD
  4. Take screenshots as evidence
  5. Upload screenshots to thoughts repo
  6. If ALL criteria pass: close the bead (epic is validated, ready for human review)
  7. If ANY criteria fail: create fix beads for failures, add them to the convoy with 'gt convoy add', create a NEW validation bead that depends on the fix beads, add that to the convoy too, then close self. The daemon handles dispatch of fix beads and the next validation bead.

This creates a self-terminating validation loop: validation only spawns new work when criteria fail, and stops when everything passes.

## Files to modify
- ~/.beads/formulas/mol-idea-to-plan.formula.toml (PRIMARY — this is the runtime copy)

## Verification
- The formula should still parse correctly
- The PRD template should render cleanly in markdown
- The create-beads step should clearly instruct the agent to create the validation bead

## PR
Create a PR on the gastown repo (github.com/steveyegge/gastown) with the changes. The formula source is at:
- gastown/crew/bridger/internal/formula/formulas/mol-idea-to-plan.formula.toml
- gastown/mayor/rig/internal/formula/formulas/mol-idea-to-plan.formula.toml  
- gastown/refinery/rig/internal/formula/formulas/mol-idea-to-plan.formula.toml
All three copies must be kept in sync.
title: Update mol-idea-to-plan: add validation criteria to PRD template + auto-create validation bead
